name: Ibiza Resort
description: World-famous party and relaxation destination located in the Mediterranean Sea.
fun_facts: Has over 50 beaches
UNESCO World Heritage site

name: The Colosseum
description: An ancient amphitheater in the center of Rome, the largest ever built.
fun_facts: Could hold an estimated 50,000 to 80,000 spectators
Used for gladiatorial contests

name: Whistler Blackcomb
description: A major ski resort in British Columbia, Canada, offering incredible winter sports.
fun_facts: Hosted the 2010 Winter Olympics
Has over 200 marked ski trails

name: Machu Picchu
description: A 15th-century Inca citadel located in the Eastern Cordillera of southern Peru.
fun_facts: Built without the use of mortar
Often referred to as the 'Lost City of the Incas'

name: Times Square
description: A major commercial intersection, tourist destination, and entertainment center in Midtown Manhattan.
fun_facts: One of the world's most visited tourist attractions
Famous for its annual New Year's Eve ball drop

name: Great Barrier Reef
description: The world's largest coral reef system composed of over 2,900 individual reefs.
fun_facts: Can be seen from outer space
Home to countless species of colorful fish and marine life

name: Kyoto Temples
description: The cultural capital of Japan, famous for its numerous classical Buddhist temples, gardens, and imperial palaces.
fun_facts: Spared from bombing during WWII due to its historic value
Features over 1,000 temples

name: Santorini Caldera
description: A stunning island in the Aegean Sea known for its white-washed, cubiform houses overlooking the ocean.
fun_facts: Formed by one of the largest volcanic eruptions in recorded history
Famous for breathtaking sunsets

name: Banff National Park
description: Canada's oldest national park, encompassing mountainous terrain, numerous glaciers, and dense coniferous forest.
fun_facts: Known for its brilliantly turquoise glacial lakes
Offers incredible wildlife viewing opportunities

name: Mount Fuji Summit
description: Japan's highest mountain and an active stratovolcano, typically only open for climbing in the summer.
fun_facts: Considered one of Japan's three holy mountains
Perfectly symmetrical cone is a well-known symbol of Japan

name: Eiffel Tower
description: A wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France.
fun_facts: Constructed from 1887 to 1889
It was initially criticized by some of France's leading artists

name: Grand Canyon
description: A steep-sided canyon carved by the Colorado River in Arizona, United States.
fun_facts: It is 277 miles long and up to 18 miles wide
Reveals nearly two billion years of Earth's geological history

name: Zermatt Ski Resort
description: A renowned alpine resort in southern Switzerland, known for skiing, climbing and hiking.
fun_facts: Lies right below the iconic, pyramid-shaped Matterhorn peak
The town is a car-free zone

name: Pyramids of Giza
description: Ancient pyramid structures located on the Giza Plateau bordering Cairo, Egypt.
fun_facts: The Great Pyramid is the oldest of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World
Built as tombs for the country's pharaohs

name: Bora Bora
description: A small South Pacific island northwest of Tahiti in French Polynesia, surrounded by sand-fringed motus.
fun_facts: Famous for its luxury aqua-centric luxury resorts
The island is heavily dependent on tourism

name: Yellowstone Caldera
description: A volcanic caldera and supervolcano in Yellowstone National Park in the Western United States.
fun_facts: Contains half of the world's hydrothermal features
Famous for the Old Faithful geyser

name: Burj Khalifa
description: A skyscraper in Dubai, United Arab Emirates, and the tallest structure and building in the world.
fun_facts: Stands at a total height of 829.8 meters
Features the world's highest observation deck

name: Stonehenge
description: A prehistoric monument on Salisbury Plain in Wiltshire, England.
fun_facts: Consists of an outer ring of vertical sarsen standing stones
Believed to have been constructed from 3000 BC to 2000 BC

name: Copacabana Beach
description: A world-famous neighborhood in the South Zone of Rio de Janeiro, known for its 4 km balneario beach.
fun_facts: Host to millions of revelers during the annual New Year's Eve celebrations
The promenade features a distinct geometric wave pattern

name: Chichen Itza
description: A large pre-Columbian city built by the Maya people of the Terminal Classic period.
fun_facts: El Castillo pyramid has 365 steps, representing the solar year
Recently closed for extensive structural preservation and archaeological surveys

name: Taj Mahal
description: An ivory-white marble mausoleum on the right bank of the river Yamuna in Agra, India.
fun_facts: Commissioned in 1632 by the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan
Attracts over 7 million visitors a year

name: Yosemite Valley
description: A glacial valley in Yosemite National Park in the western Sierra Nevada mountains of California.
fun_facts: Famous for its giant sequoia groves and granite cliffs like El Capitan
A UNESCO World Heritage site since 1984

name: Marina Bay Sands
description: An integrated resort fronting Marina Bay in Singapore, known for its striking boat-shaped rooftop design.
fun_facts: Features a 150-meter infinity pool on the roof
The resort includes a museum, casino, and massive shopping mall

name: Mount Kilimanjaro
description: A dormant volcano in Tanzania and the highest single free-standing mountain above sea level in the world.
fun_facts: It has three distinct volcanic cones: Kibo, Mawenzi, and Shira
Can be climbed without technical mountaineering equipment

name: Seminyak Beach
description: A mixed tourist and residential area on the west coast of Bali, known for its luxury hotels, villas, and spas.
fun_facts: Famous for spectacular ocean sunsets and upscale beach clubs
Features some of the best high-end dining on the island

name: Louvre Museum
description: The world's most-visited museum and a historic monument in Paris, France.
fun_facts: Home to the Mona Lisa and the Venus de Milo
The iconic glass pyramid was added to the main courtyard in 1989

name: Chamonix Mont-Blanc
description: A resort area near the junction of France, Switzerland, and Italy, situated at the base of Mont Blanc.
fun_facts: Hosted the first Winter Olympics in 1924
Renowned worldwide for extreme skiing and mountaineering

name: Angkor Wat
description: A temple complex in Cambodia and the largest religious monument in the world by land area.
fun_facts: Originally constructed as a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Vishnu
Appears on the national flag of Cambodia

name: Maldives Atolls
description: An archipelagic state in South Asia, situated in the Indian Ocean, famous for its overwater bungalows.
fun_facts: It is the world's lowest-lying country
Composed of 26 ring-shaped atolls made up of more than 1,000 coral islands

name: Petra
description: A historical and archaeological city in southern Jordan, famous for its rock-cut architecture.
fun_facts: Also known as the 'Rose City' due to the color of the stone
Access to the city is through a 1.2-kilometer-long gorge called the Siq

name: Serengeti National Park
description: A vast ecosystem in east-central Africa spanning northern Tanzania, known for its massive annual migration of wildebeest and zebra.
fun_facts: Hosts the largest terrestrial mammal migration in the world
The name means 'endless plains' in the Maasai language

name: Venice Canals
description: A city in northeastern Italy built on a group of 118 small islands separated by canals and linked by over 400 bridges.
fun_facts: There are no roads in the city center, only canals and walking paths
Gondolas have been used to navigate the city since the 11th century

name: Waikiki Beach
description: A neighborhood of Honolulu on the south shore of the island of Oʻahu, world-famous for its white sand beach and surf.
fun_facts: Historically a retreat for Hawaiian royalty in the 1800s
The name Waikiki means 'spouting fresh water' in the Hawaiian language

name: Great Wall of China
description: A series of ancient fortifications built across the historical northern borders of ancient Chinese states and Imperial China.
fun_facts: Stretches over 21,000 kilometers in total length
It is a myth that it can be seen from space with the naked eye

name: Shibuya Crossing
description: A popular scramble crossing in Shibuya, Tokyo, often considered the busiest intersection in the world.
fun_facts: Up to 3,000 people can cross the street during a single light change
Features the famous Hachikō statue at one of its main exits

name: Torres del Paine
description: A national park encompassing mountains, glaciers, lakes, and rivers in southern Chilean Patagonia.
fun_facts: Known for its three distinctive granite peaks shaped by glacial ice
Features extreme and rapidly changing weather conditions

name: Aspen Snowmass
description: A winter resort complex located in Pitkin County, Colorado, featuring four separate skiing and snowboarding areas.
fun_facts: Snowmass is the largest of the four mountains, larger than the other three combined
A premier destination for the Winter X Games

name: Galápagos Islands
description: A volcanic archipelago in the Pacific Ocean, considered one of the world's foremost destinations for wildlife-viewing.
fun_facts: Famous for its large number of endemic species studied by Charles Darwin
Located exactly on the Equator

name: Acropolis of Athens
description: An ancient citadel located on a rocky outcrop above the city of Athens, containing the remains of several historically significant buildings.
fun_facts: The Parthenon is the most famous building in the complex
Has withstood bombardments, earthquakes, and vandalism over 2,500 years

name: Salar de Uyuni
description: The world's largest salt flat, spanning over 10,000 square kilometers in southwestern Bolivia.
fun_facts: Becomes a giant mirror reflecting the sky during the rainy season
Contains a large percentage of the world's known lithium reserves

name: Iguazu Falls
description: The largest waterfall system in the world, located on the border of the Argentine province of Misiones and the Brazilian state of Paraná.
fun_facts: Consists of 275 individual waterfalls
Eleanor Roosevelt reportedly exclaimed 'Poor Niagara!' upon seeing it

name: Mount Rushmore
description: A colossal sculpture carved into the granite face of Mount Rushmore in the Black Hills region of South Dakota.
fun_facts: Features the 60-foot heads of four US presidents
Took 14 years and nearly 400 workers to complete

name: Mykonos Island
description: A Greek island in the Cyclades group, known for its vibrant nightlife, beautiful beaches, and iconic 16th-century windmills.
fun_facts: According to mythology, it was formed from the petrified bodies of giants killed by Hercules
Features a famous neighborhood known as 'Little Venice'

name: Alhambra
description: A palace and fortress complex located in Granada, Andalusia, Spain, renowned for its stunning Islamic architecture.
fun_facts: The name translates to 'The Red One' in Arabic
It was originally constructed as a small fortress in 889 AD

name: Tulum Ruins
description: The site of a pre-Columbian Mayan walled city serving as a major port, situated on tall cliffs along the east coast of the Yucatán Peninsula.
fun_facts: One of the last cities built and inhabited by the Maya
The only Mayan city built directly on the coast

name: Milford Sound
description: A fiord in the southwest of New Zealand's South Island, known for towering Mitre Peak, rainforests and waterfalls.
fun_facts: Described by Rudyard Kipling as the 'eighth wonder of the world'
Receives nearly 7 meters of rainfall annually

name: Vatican City
description: An independent city-state enclaved within Rome, Italy, and the headquarters of the Roman Catholic Church.
fun_facts: The smallest country in the world by both area and population
Home to the Sistine Chapel ceiling painted by Michelangelo

name: Niseko United
description: A premier ski resort area in Hokkaido, Japan, famous globally for its abundant, high-quality powder snow.
fun_facts: Averages an incredible 15 meters of snowfall per year
Night skiing is exceptionally popular due to stadium-style lighting

name: Table Mountain
description: A prominent flat-topped mountain forming a prominent landmark overlooking the city of Cape Town in South Africa.
fun_facts: Often covered by a 'tablecloth' of orographic clouds
One of the oldest mountains in the world, estimated to be 260 million years old

name: Ha Long Bay
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site in northeast Vietnam, known for its emerald waters and thousands of towering limestone islands topped by rainforests.
fun_facts: The name translates to 'descending dragon'
Features numerous massive caves hidden inside the limestone karsts
